title: festuca paniculata subsp. moleroi
intro: Festuca paniculata subsp. moleroi, also known as Molero's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Festuca paniculata subsp. moleroi include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as a natural expectorant, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
